source.book_title: DBT Skills Training Handouts and Worksheets, Second Edition
source.author: Marsha M. Linehan
source.extraction_method: PDF outline/table-of-contents assisted parsing with paraphrased summaries and app schemas
source.copyright_note: This JSON is an original structured index and paraphrased schema. It intentionally does not reproduce full handout or worksheet text from the book.

modules.module: General Skills: Orientation and Analyzing Behavior
modules.short: Orientation to DBT skills training, goals, assumptions, behavior analysis, and problem-solving choices.
aims: Clarify what skills training is for and how it is practiced.
Frame problems as changeable chains of causes, consequences, and missing links.
Build a nonjudgmental foundation for learning new behavior.

modules.short: Skills for noticing the present moment, entering Wise Mind, reducing judgment, and acting effectively.
aims: Observe, describe, and participate in present experience.
Balance doing and being, acceptance and change, and opposite truths.
sections: Handouts for Other Perspectives on Mindfulness Skills
Worksheets for Other Perspectives on Mindfulness Skills

modules.short: Skills for asking, saying no, keeping relationships, keeping self-respect, validating, and changing behavior.
aims: Ask for what is wanted and set limits while protecting the relationship and self-respect.
Build, maintain, and end relationships skillfully.
Use dialectics, validation, reinforcement, extinction, and problem solving in relationships.
sections: Handouts for Building Relationships and Ending Destructive Ones
Handouts for Goals and Factors That Interfere
Handouts for Walking the Middle Path
Worksheets for Building Relationships and Ending Destructive Ones
Worksheets for Goals and Factors That Interfere
Worksheets for Walking the Middle Path

modules.short: Skills for understanding emotions, changing emotional responses, reducing vulnerability, and managing intense emotions.
aims: Name emotions and understand their prompting events, meanings, body cues, actions, and aftereffects.
Check facts, choose opposite action or problem solving, and reduce emotion intensity.
Build a life worth living through positives, values, mastery, cope ahead, body care, sleep, and mindful emotion practice.
sections: Handouts for Managing Really Difficult Emotions
Handouts for Reducing Vulnerability to Emotion Mind
Handouts for Understanding and Naming Emotions

Worksheets for Reducing Vulnerability to Emotion Mind

modules.short: Skills for surviving crisis, resisting destructive urges, accepting reality, and handling addiction-related crises.
aims: Survive crisis moments without making the situation worse.
Accept reality when change is not immediately possible in order to reduce suffering.
Use abstinence, clear mind, reinforcement, and environment redesign when addictive patterns are active.
sections: Handouts for Skills When the Crisis Is Addiction
Worksheets for Skills When the Crisis Is Addiction

DEAR MAN.purpose: Objectives effectiveness: ask, say no, or negotiate while staying focused.

letters.D: Describe the situation briefly and concretely.
letters.R: Reinforce the other person for responding helpfully.
letters.M: Stay mindful of the goal; return to the point when pulled away.
letters.A2: Appear confident enough to be taken seriously.

GIVE.purpose: Relationship effectiveness: protect or strengthen the relationship during interaction.

letters.G: Be gentle in manner and wording.
letters.I: Act interested in the other person.
letters.V: Validate the other person’s experience where valid.
letters.E: Use an easy manner when it fits the situation.

FAST.purpose: Self-respect effectiveness: keep self-respect while interacting.

letters.F: Be fair to yourself and the other person.
letters.A: Do not over-apologize for existing, needing, or having limits.
letters.T: Be truthful and avoid exaggeration, helplessness, or manipulation.

STOP.purpose: Pause before acting in crisis so urges do not take over.

letters.S: Stop and do not immediately react.
letters.T: Take a step back physically or mentally.
letters.O: Observe what is happening inside and around you.
letters.P: Proceed mindfully based on goals, facts, and Wise Mind.

TIP.purpose: Use body-based methods to quickly reduce extreme arousal.

letters.T: Change body temperature safely, often with cold water or cooling.
letters.I: Use intense brief exercise when medically safe.
letters.P: Use paced breathing and paired muscle relaxation.

ACCEPTS.purpose: Distract skillfully from crisis urges long enough to avoid making things worse.

IMPROVE.purpose: Improve the current moment when the problem cannot be solved immediately.

letters.P: Prayer or connection to something larger when it fits the person’s values.

PLEASE.purpose: Reduce biological vulnerability to emotion mind through body care.

letters.PL: Treat physical illness and attend to medical needs.
letters.A: Avoid mood-altering substances unless prescribed and used as directed.
letters.E2: Get exercise appropriate to ability and health.

ABC.purpose: Build resilience through positives, mastery, and preparation.

letters.A: Accumulate positive emotions in the short and long term.
letters.B: Build mastery through doable actions that increase competence.
letters.C: Cope ahead by rehearsing skillful responses to predictable stressors.
